query: US spot Bitcoin ETF net flows late June 2026 and early July 2026, weekly trend from Farside Investors and SoSoValue. Are net outflows continuing into the week of June 29-July 3 2026? What is the Bitcoin price trend in late June 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the bottom line:

**Were net outflows continuing into the week of June 29–July 3, 2026?** — **Yes, on a weekly basis.** The week posted a net outflow of **–$526.64 million**, extending the streak to **8 consecutive weeks** (over $8.2B cumulative). However, the *daily* trend reversed at the very end: after three straight outflow days (Jun 29: –$231M, Jun 30: –$223M, Jul 1: –$295M), **July 2 saw a +$221.72M inflow** — the largest single-day inflow in ~2 months — snapping a 10-day daily outflow streak. July 3 markets were closed for U.S. Independence Day.

**Bitcoin price trend in late June 2026** — Firmly **downward, bottoming**. BTC opened June near $73,500, fell below $60,000 mid-month (first time since late 2024), and hit **~21-month lows under $58,000** during the June 29–July 3 window ($58,503.73 on June 30 per Fortune). It then rebounded to **~$61,700 by July 3** and recovered above **$64,000 by July 7** (~6% weekly gain) — the recovery coinciding with the ETF flow reversal.

Context: June 2026 was the **worst month on record** for US spot Bitcoin ETFs (~$4.06B outflows per SoSoValue, some providers cite up to $4.5B). Full structured findings with all data points and sources have been delivered.

sources.snippet: US-listed spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded ~$4.06 billion in net outflows in June 2026 per SoSoValue, worst month since launch, eclipsing $3.56B record from Feb 2025.

key_facts: Week of June 29–July 3, 2026 US spot Bitcoin ETF net flow: -$526.64 million (net outflow), the 8th consecutive week of outflows.
Daily flows that week: Jun 29 -$231.10M, Jun 30 -$222.64M, Jul 1 -$294.62M, Jul 2 +$221.72M; Jul 3 markets closed (US Independence Day).
July 2 inflow of +$221.72M was the largest single-day inflow in ~2 months and ended a 10-day daily outflow streak that had drained ~$2.73B.
Eight-week cumulative outflow streak totaled over $8.2 billion.
June 2026 was the worst month on record for US spot Bitcoin ETFs: ~$4.06B net outflows per SoSoValue (some providers cite up to $4.5B), beating the prior $3.56B record (Feb 2025).
Bitcoin fell below $60,000 mid-June 2026 for the first time since late 2024 and hit ~21-month lows under $58,000 during the June 29–July 3 week.
BTC spot price June 30, 2026 was $58,503.73 (Fortune, 9:15am ET).
BTC rebounded to ~$61,700 by July 3 (+2.7% on day) and recovered above $64,000 by July 7 (~6% weekly gain).
Bitcoin opened June 2026 near $73,500; it had peaked around $126,000 in October 2025.
Sell-off drivers included institutional distribution, record ETF outflows, and Strategy (MSTR) BTC sales.
